title: scan_block saves tree_state before completing witness upserts; partial failure leaves inconsistent state
description: In scanner.rs scan_block, save_tree_state is called before the per-note upsert_witness loop completes. If upsert_witness fails for any note after the tree snapshot is written (e.g. DB error mid-loop), scan_tip is not advanced (good) but tree_state is already at the new height. On the next scan, the scanner re-processes the block, appending the same commitments to the in-memory tree again — the tree now has doubled commitments and diverges from the chain. Witnesses forked from this corrupted tree produce invalid Merkle paths and spend proofs will fail.
